title: Senator Dave Cortese (D-Silicon Valley), who serves on the Senate Budget and Fiscal Review Committee as well as Chair of the Senate Labor, Public Employment and Retirement Committe

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Legislative Analyst's Office projects California's budget surplus to be $31 billion for the 2022-23 fiscal year.",
    "California's budget surplus presents a critical opportunity to stabilize and strengthen local communities rebuilding from the pandemic.",
    "Investments in public health infrastructure are essential for COVID-19 response, testing, and vaccinations.",
    "The expansion of Medi-Cal coverage to all income-eligible Californians, regardless of immigration status, is proposed.",
    "Early learning is key to a child's successful learning outcomes and lifelong development.",
    "Increased funding is proposed for early education, including universal transitional kindergarten and the State Preschool Program.",
    "The Inclusive Early Education Expansion Program aims to support inclusive education for students with disabilities.",
    "Adding 10,000 more mental health clinicians to the school system is proposed to support students' mental health.",
    "Increased investment in school-based mental health centers and support systems is prioritized.",
    "California experienced its second driest year on record in 2021 due to worsening climate change impacts.",
    "California is currently facing its worst drought since the late 1800s.",
    "Proposed spending of $750 million for water conservation and replenishment efforts is aimed at aiding communities affected by drought.",
    "Support for transitioning to clean energy sources includes education, training, and workforce transition programs for displaced oil and gas workers.",
    "Investment to tackle homelessness includes a total of $9 billion towards housing and $8 billion towards addressing homelessness.",
    "It is proposed to shore up reserve accounts in preparation for tighter budget years ahead."
  ]
}
```
